permanent_tropes.label: Credentialed Liar
permanent_tropes.description: Medical insider whose professional authority collapses under the weight of their deception. Their credentials are the weapon and the wound.

permanent_tropes.label: Institutional Gaslighting
permanent_tropes.description: The system reframes the narrator's legitimate concern as instability. The institution protects itself by pathologising dissent.

rotating_tropes.label: A — Medication as Unreliable Witness
rotating_tropes.description: Medication blurs the narrator's capacity to distinguish memory from pharmacological artifact. The reader cannot trust what the narrator remembers under chemical influence.

rotating_tropes.label: B — Complicit Witness
rotating_tropes.description: A secondary character who witnessed the truth but chose complicity. Their silence is load-bearing to the misdirection architecture.

rotating_tropes.label: C — Retrospective Confession
rotating_tropes.description: The narrator has already confessed before the story begins. The novel is the confession. The reader discovers this structural truth late.

rotating_tropes.label: D — Reserved
rotating_tropes.description: Locked until after Book 3. Requires editorial unlock with written rationale.

rolling_overlap_pattern.book_4_note: Blocked until Trope D defined and editorially unlocked

thematic_dna.label: Protagonist Complicity
thematic_dna.description: The protagonist is always complicit before they are victimised. This is not optional — it is the moral engine of every Leila Rex novel.

thematic_dna.label: Medical Setting as Mechanism
thematic_dna.description: Medicine is not backdrop — it is weaponised. The clinical environment enables both the crime and its concealment.

thematic_dna.label: Moral Ambiguity Without Moral Relativism
thematic_dna.description: The reader is forced into moral discomfort, but the novel does not pretend all positions are equal. There is a moral floor.

narrator_architecture.description: Narrator unreliability is load-bearing to the twist architecture. The narrator's self-deception is not decoration — it is the structural mechanism that makes the twist land.
